children.text: to research, develop, and operationally test intelligent transportation systems; and
to provide technical assistance in the nationwide application of those systems as a component of the surface transportation systems of the United States.

children.heading: .—Policy
children.label: b .—Policy
children.text: Intelligent transportation system research projects and operational tests funded pursuant to sections 512 through 518 shall encourage and not displace public-private partnerships or private sector investment in those tests and projects.

children.heading: .—Cooperation With Governmental, Private, and Educational Entities
children.label: c .—Cooperation With Governmental, Private, and Educational Entities
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out the intelligent transportation system program in cooperation with State and local governments and other public entities, the private sector firms of the United States, the Federal laboratories, and institutions of higher education, including historically Black colleges and universities and other minority institutions of higher education.

children.heading: .—Consultation With Federal Officials
children.label: d .—Consultation With Federal Officials
children.text: In carrying out the intelligent transportation system program, the Secretary shall consult with the heads of other Federal agencies, as appropriate.

children.heading: .—Technical Assistance, Training, and Information
children.label: e .—Technical Assistance, Training, and Information
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance, training, and information to State and local governments seeking to implement, operate, maintain, or evaluate intelligent transportation system technologies and services.

children.heading: .—Transportation Planning
children.label: f .—Transportation Planning
children.text: The Secretary may provide funding to support adequate consideration of transportation systems management and operations, including intelligent transportation systems, within metropolitan and statewide transportation planning processes.

children.label: A
children.text: maintain a repository for technical and safety data collected as a result of federally sponsored projects carried out under sections 512 through 518; and

children.label: B
children.text: make, on request, that information (except for proprietary information and data) readily available to all users of the repository at an appropriate cost.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into an agreement with a third party for the maintenance of the repository for technical and safety data under paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: .—Federal financial assistance
children.label: B .—Federal financial assistance
children.text: If the Secretary enters into an agreement with an entity for the maintenance of the repository, the entity shall be eligible for Federal financial assistance under this section.

children.heading: .—Availability of information
children.label: 3 .—Availability of information
children.text: Information in the repository shall not be subject to sections 552 and 555 of title 5, United States Code.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an Advisory Committee (referred to in this subsection as the “Advisory Committee”) to advise the Secretary on carrying out sections 512 through 518.

children.label: A
children.text: a representative from a State highway department;

children.label: B
children.text: a representative from a local highway department who is not from a metropolitan planning organization;

children.label: C
children.text: a representative from a State, local, or regional transit agency;

children.label: D
children.text: a representative from a State, local, or regional wildlife, land use, or resource management agency;

children.label: E
children.text: a representative from a metropolitan planning organization;

children.label: F
children.text: a representative of a national transit association;

children.label: G
children.text: a representative of a national, State, or local transportation agency or association;

children.label: H
children.text: a private sector user of intelligent transportation system technologies;

children.label: I
children.text: a private sector developer of intelligent transportation system technologies, which may include emerging vehicle technologies;

children.label: J
children.text: an academic researcher with expertise in computer science or another information science field related to intelligent transportation systems, and who is not an expert on transportation issues;

children.label: K
children.text: an academic researcher who is a civil engineer;

children.label: L
children.text: an academic researcher who is a social scientist with expertise in transportation issues;

children.label: M
children.text: an academic researcher who is a biological or ecological scientist with expertise in transportation issues;

children.label: N
children.text: a representative from a nonprofit group representing the intelligent transportation system industry;

children.label: O
children.text: a representative from a public interest group concerned with safety;

children.label: P
children.text: a representative of a labor organization;

children.label: Q
children.text: a representative of a mobility-providing entity;

children.label: R
children.text: an expert in traffic management;

children.label: S
children.text: a representative from a public interest group concerned with the impact of the transportation system on land use and residential patterns;

children.label: T
children.text: a representative from a public interest group concerned with the impact of the transportation system on terrestrial and aquatic species and the habitat of those species; and

children.label: U
children.text: members with expertise in planning, safety, telecommunications, and operations;

children.label: V
children.text: an expert in cybersecurity; and

children.label: W
children.text: an automobile manufacturer.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term of a member of the Advisory Committee shall be 3 years.

children.label: i
children.text: may be reappointed; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the member is not reappointed under clause (i), may serve until a new member is appointed.

children.label: A
children.text: shall convene not less frequently than twice each year; and

children.label: B
children.text: may convene with the use of remote video conference technology.

children.label: A
children.text: Provide input into the development of the intelligent transportation system aspects of the strategic plan under .section 6503 of title 49

children.label: i
children.text: whether these activities are likely to advance either the state-of-the-practice or state-of-the-art in intelligent transportation systems;

children.label: ii
children.text: whether the intelligent transportation system technologies are likely to be deployed by users, and if not, to determine the barriers to deployment; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the appropriate roles for government and the private sector in investing in the programs, research, and technologies being considered.

children.label: A
children.text: all recommendations made by the Advisory Committee during the preceding calendar year;

children.label: B
children.text: an explanation of the manner in which the Secretary has implemented those recommendations; and

children.label: C
children.text: for recommendations not implemented, the reasons for rejecting the recommendations.

children.heading: .—Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5, united states code
children.label: 7 .—Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5, united states code
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall be subject to chapter 10 of title 5, United States Code.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall issue guidelines and requirements for the reporting and evaluation of operational tests and deployment projects carried out under sections 512 through 518.

children.heading: .—Objectivity and independence
children.label: B .—Objectivity and independence
children.text: The guidelines and requirements issued under subparagraph (A) shall include provisions to ensure the objectivity and independence of the reporting entity so as to avoid any real or apparent conflict of interest or potential influence on the outcome by parties to any such test or deployment project or by any other formal evaluation carried out under sections 512 through 518.

children.heading: .—Funding
children.label: C .—Funding
children.text: The guidelines and requirements issued under subparagraph (A) shall establish reporting funding levels based on the size and scope of each test or project that ensure adequate reporting of the results of the test or project.

children.heading: .—Special rule
children.label: 2 .—Special rule
children.text: Any survey, questionnaire, or interview that the Secretary considers necessary to carry out the reporting of any test, deployment project, or program assessment activity under sections 512 through 518 shall not be subject to chapter 35 of title 44, United States Code.
